diseases.name: Fungal Infection
diseases.brief: A skin disease caused by a fungus, commonly affecting moist areas of the body like feet, groin, or skin folds.
precautions: Keep skin clean and dry
Change clothes and socks daily
Avoid sharing personal items
Wear loose, breathable cotton clothing

diseases.name: Allergy
diseases.brief: An immune system reaction to a foreign substance (allergen) that is typically harmless to most people.
causes: Airborne allergens (pollen, dust mites, mold, pet dander)
precautions: Identify and avoid known allergens
Keep indoor air clean using HEPA filters
Wash bedding regularly in hot water
Carry prescribed antihistamines
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: GERD
diseases.brief: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease is a chronic digestive disease where stomach acid flows back into the food pipe, irritating the lining.
causes: Weakening of the lower esophageal sphincter
Eating large meals late at night
precautions: Avoid trigger foods (fatty, spicy, caffeine)
Eat smaller, more frequent meals
Do not lie down immediately after eating
Elevate the head of your bed
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Chronic Cholestasis
diseases.brief: A condition where the flow of bile from the liver is reduced or stalled over a prolonged period.
causes: Bile duct obstruction or genetic defects
precautions: Follow a low-fat diet
Avoid alcohol completely
Monitor liver function regularly
Take vitamin supplements if recommended by a doctor
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Drug Reaction
diseases.brief: An adverse and unintended response or allergic reaction to a properly administered medication.
causes: Immune system hypersensitivity to a drug
precautions: Stop the suspected medication immediately
Seek urgent medical care if breathing is affected
Inform all healthcare providers of your drug allergies
Always carry an allergy card
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Peptic Ulcer Disease
diseases.brief: Sores that develop on the inside lining of the stomach, small intestine, or esophagus.
causes: Long-term use of NSAID painkillers (aspirin, ibuprofen)
precautions: Limit the use of NSAID painkillers
Avoid spicy, acidic, and fried foods
Quit smoking and limit alcohol
Manage stress levels
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: AIDS
diseases.brief: Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome is a chronic, potentially life-threatening condition caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV).
causes: Transmission of HIV via unprotected sexual contact
precautions: Practice safe sex (use condoms)
Never share needles or syringes
Screen blood before transfusion
Take Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) if at high risk
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Diabetes
diseases.brief: A chronic metabolic disorder characterized by high blood glucose levels due to problems with insulin production or usage.
precautions: Maintain a balanced, low-sugar diet
Engage in regular physical exercise
Monitor blood glucose levels consistently
Take prescribed medication or insulin punctually
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Gastroenteritis
diseases.brief: An intestinal infection marked by diarrhea, cramps, nausea, vomiting, and fever, often called stomach flu.
precautions: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions (ORS)
Wash hands thoroughly before eating
Consume fresh, well-cooked food
Avoid contaminated or street food

diseases.name: Bronchial Asthma
diseases.brief: A chronic respiratory condition where the airways become inflamed, narrow, and swell, producing extra mucus.
causes: Cold air or sudden weather changes
precautions: Avoid known triggers and smoke
Always keep a rescue inhaler nearby
Take long-term controller medications as prescribed
Get annual flu vaccinations

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.brief: A chronic condition where the force of the blood against the artery walls is consistently too high (High Blood Pressure).
precautions: Reduce dietary sodium (salt) intake
Exercise regularly (at least 30 minutes a day)
Manage stress through relaxation techniques
Limit alcohol and quit smoking

diseases.name: Migraine
diseases.brief: A neurological condition characterized by intense, debilitating throbbing headaches, usually on one side of the head.
causes: Irregular sleep patterns or specific foods
precautions: Maintain a regular sleep schedule
Identify and avoid dietary triggers (caffeine, aged cheese)
Stay hydrated
Rest in a dark, quiet room during an attack

diseases.name: Cervical Spondylosis
diseases.brief: Age-related wear and tear affecting the spinal disks in the neck.
causes: Aging and degeneration of spinal disks
Prolonged poor posture (looking down at screens)
precautions: Maintain good posture while sitting and working
Do regular neck stretching exercises
Use an ergonomic chair and pillow
Take frequent breaks from screens

diseases.name: Paralysis (Brain Hemorrhage)
diseases.brief: Loss of muscle function in part of the body, caused here by bleeding inside the brain disrupting neural pathways.
precautions: Strictly manage and control blood pressure
Avoid smoking and heavy drinking
Seek immediate emergency care at the first sign of stroke
Engage in regular physical therapy during recovery

diseases.name: Jaundice
diseases.brief: A yellow staining of the skin and eyes caused by high levels of bilirubin in the blood.
precautions: Eat a light, easily digestible diet
Avoid alcohol and fatty foods entirely
Drink boiled or purified water
Get adequate rest to support liver recovery
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Malaria
diseases.brief: A life-threatening disease caused by plasmodium parasites transmitted through the bite of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
causes: Bite of an infected female Anopheles mosquito
precautions: Use mosquito nets and insect repellent
Wear protective clothing covering skin
Eliminate stagnant water around the home
Take prophylactic medications if traveling to endemic areas
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Chicken Pox
diseases.brief: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy, blister-like rash all over the body.
causes: Direct contact with blisters or respiratory droplets of an infected person
precautions: Isolate the infected individual
Get vaccinated (Varicella vaccine)
Avoid scratching blisters to prevent scarring/infection
Keep skin cool and clean
diseases.disclaimer: WARNING: This is an automated machine-learning response and may contain mistakes. Do not rely on this for an official diagnosis. Please consult a qualified doctor for proper evaluation and treatment.

diseases.name: Dengue
diseases.brief: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing severe flu-like symptoms and potentially fatal complications like hemorrhagic fever.
causes: Bite of an infected Aedes mosquito infected with the dengue virus
precautions: Prevent mosquito breeding by draining stagnant water
Use mosquito screens and repellents
Stay hydrated with fluids and electrolytes
Avoid taking aspirin or ibuprofen (use paracetamol instead)

diseases.name: Typhoid
diseases.brief: A life-threatening bacterial infection causing high fever and severe gastrointestinal symptoms.
causes: Ingestion of contaminated food or water
precautions: Drink safe, boiled, or bottled water
Eat hot, freshly cooked food
Maintain strict hand hygiene
Get vaccinated before traveling to high-risk zones

diseases.name: Hepatitis A/B/C/D/E
diseases.brief: Inflammation of the liver caused by various hepatitis viruses (A to E), leading to liver damage ranging from acute to chronic.
causes: Contaminated food and water (Hep A, E)
Infected blood, semen, or bodily fluids (Hep B, C, D)
precautions: Get vaccinated against Hepatitis A and B
Consume clean, hygienic food and water
Avoid sharing needles or razors
Ensure sterile equipment is used for medical procedures
Practice safe sex

diseases.name: Tuberculosis
diseases.brief: A serious, highly infectious bacterial disease that mainly affects the lungs but can spread to other organs.
causes: Inhaling microscopic droplets from the cough or sneeze of an infected person
precautions: Complete the entire long-term course of prescribed antibiotics
Wear masks in crowded space if infected
Ensure proper room ventilation
Get BCG vaccination for infants

diseases.name: Common Cold
diseases.brief: A viral infection of your upper respiratory tract, primarily affecting your nose and throat.
causes: Airborne droplets or touching contaminated surfaces
precautions: Wash hands frequently with soap and water
Avoid close contact with infected individuals
Stay hydrated and rest well
Cover your mouth and nose when sneezing

diseases.name: Pneumonia
diseases.brief: An infection that inflames the air sacs (alveoli) in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
precautions: Get the pneumococcal vaccine and annual flu shot
Practice good hand hygiene
Quit smoking to protect lung defenses
Seek prompt treatment for lingering respiratory infections

diseases.name: Heart Attack
diseases.brief: A medical emergency where the flow of blood to a section of the heart muscle becomes blocked, causing tissue damage.
precautions: Maintain a heart-healthy, low-cholesterol diet
Exercise regularly and maintain a healthy weight
Do not smoke and minimize stress
Take prescribed blood pressure or cholesterol medicines strictly

diseases.name: Varicose Veins
diseases.brief: Gnarled, enlarged veins, most commonly appearing in the legs and feet due to weakened vein valves.
precautions: Avoid standing or sitting for long periods without moving
Exercise regularly to improve leg circulation
Elevate your legs when resting
Wear compression stockings

diseases.name: Hypothyroidism
diseases.brief: A condition in which the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough crucial thyroid hormones.
causes: Surgical removal of thyroid or radiation therapy
precautions: Take prescribed thyroid hormone replacement medication consistently
Get regular blood tests to monitor TSH levels
Maintain an adequate dietary intake of iodine
Eat a fiber-rich diet to combat constipation

diseases.name: Hyperthyroidism
diseases.brief: A condition occurring when the thyroid gland produces excessive amounts of the hormone thyroxine.
precautions: Follow treatment plan (anti-thyroid drugs, radioactive iodine)
Monitor heart health regularly
Avoid excess iodine-rich foods like seaweed
Manage stress and get sufficient rest

diseases.name: Arthritis
diseases.brief: Inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility that typically worsens with age.
causes: Wear and tear of joint cartilage (Osteoarthritis)
Autoimmune attack on joint linings (Rheumatoid arthritis)
precautions: Engage in low-impact exercises (swimming, cycling)
Maintain a healthy weight to reduce joint pressure
Apply hot/cold packs to alleviate joint discomfort
Eat an anti-inflammatory diet rich in Omega-3

diseases.name: Psoriasis
diseases.brief: A chronic autoimmune skin disease that causes rapid buildup of skin cells, leading to scaling and inflammation.
causes: Autoimmune system acceleration of skin cell growth
Triggers like stress, skin injuries, or certain medications
precautions: Keep skin thoroughly moisturized
Avoid known skin irritants or harsh soaps
Manage mental stress levels
Get moderate, controlled sun exposure

diseases.name: Impetigo
diseases.brief: A highly contagious bacterial skin infection that causes sores and blisters, predominantly in infants and children.
causes: Staphylococcus aureus or Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria
Bacteria entering through broken skin (scratches, insect bites)
precautions: Gently wash infected sores with mild soap and water
Keep sores loosely covered to prevent bacterial spread
Wash the infected person's clothes and towels separately
Keep fingernails trimmed short to prevent scratching
